questions.question: Explain why curved surface area is used to calculate the amount of paint required.
questions.answer: Paint is applied only on the exposed curved surfaces, not on hidden or base surfaces.

questions.question: Why is the base area not included in CSA?
questions.answer: Because CSA considers only the curved portion of the solid.

questions.question: Explain why TSA of combined solids is not the sum of individual TSAs.
questions.answer: Some surfaces disappear when solids are joined together.

questions.question: What is the importance of slant height in a cone?
questions.answer: Slant height is required to find the curved surface area of a cone.

questions.question: Why are volumes of combined solids added?
questions.answer: No part of volume disappears when solids are combined.

questions.question: Explain the shape of a tent.
questions.answer: A tent is usually a cylinder surmounted by a cone.

questions.question: Why is the base of a tent not included in area calculation?
questions.answer: Because the base rests on the ground and is not covered with canvas.

questions.question: Explain why capacity of a glass is less than its apparent volume.
questions.answer: Because of the hemispherical raised portion at the bottom.

questions.question: Why should radii be equal while joining solids?
questions.answer: To ensure smooth and perfect joining without gaps.

questions.question: Explain the formula for slant height of a cone.
questions.answer: It is derived using Pythagoras theorem: l = √(r² + h²).

questions.question: Why is volume measured in cubic units?
questions.answer: Because volume measures three-dimensional space.

questions.question: Why is π used in circular solids?
questions.answer: Because circular measurements involve circumference and area.

questions.question: Explain why a sphere has no edges.
questions.answer: Because its surface is completely curved.

questions.question: Why is sphere considered the most efficient shape?
questions.answer: It encloses maximum volume for minimum surface area.

questions.question: Why is base area subtracted in hollow solids?
questions.answer: Because the hollowed portion removes material.

questions.question: Explain the structure of a medicine capsule.
questions.answer: It consists of a cylinder with hemispheres at both ends.

questions.question: Why are hemispherical ends used in tanks?
questions.answer: To reduce stress and improve strength.

questions.question: Why is TSA used for insulation problems?
questions.answer: Because heat transfer depends on exposed surface area.

questions.question: Explain why cone volume is one-third of cylinder volume.
questions.answer: Due to gradual tapering of the cone.

questions.question: Why is height important in volume calculation?
questions.answer: Height determines vertical space occupied.

questions.question: Why is curved surface smoother than flat surfaces?
questions.answer: Because it has no edges or corners.

questions.question: Explain why hollowing reduces mass.
questions.answer: Because material is removed from the solid.

questions.question: Why is volume conserved when solids are combined?
questions.answer: Because no space is lost during joining.

questions.question: Why are cylindrical shapes preferred for storage?
questions.answer: They provide large volume with manageable surface area.

questions.question: Explain the term apparent capacity.
questions.answer: It is the volume calculated without considering raised or hollow parts.
